title: aquilegia vulgaris subsp. nigricans
intro:
Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. nigricans, also known as the Common Columbine or Black Columbine, is a flowering plant species within the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its delicate, nodding bell-shaped flowers in a range of colors including red, pink, yellow, and white, often with a dark purple or black spur.

The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids.

The health benefits of Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. nigricans include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use as a treatment for skin conditions like eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of ailments.

In addition, the plant has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, such as indigestion and diarrhea, and its antiseptic properties make it useful for wound healing and disinfecting.
